docs.title: Welcome to jimmy's documentation!
docs.text: jimmy is a tool to import your notes from different formats to Joplin.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
Download jimmy here: Linux | Windows | MacOS

Note

The MacOS app is untested.

Alternative installation options:

  1. CLI app: jimmy-cli-*. Available at the release page.
  2. Clone the repository and use it from python by python src/jimmy_cli.py.
  3. GUI app (experimental). Can be used from python python src/jimmy_gui.py or built manually by python -m PyInstaller jimmy_gui.spec.

docs.title: Quickstart
docs.text:
1. This script requires that the webclipper in Joplin is running. It will connect to Joplin at the first execution.
  2. Import from text files or import from specific apps, like Google Keep
  3. Verify that everything was imported properly. The imported notes should be available in a new Joplin notebook named like YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS - Import.

docs.title: Filters
docs.text: There are some simple filters to select which data to import. For more complex filtering, a separate plugin or script should be used.

docs.title: Available Filters
docs.text:
All filters can be used with wildcard. The filters are case-sensitive.

  • Exclude or include specific notes (--exclude-notes, --include-notes)
  • Exclude or include notes with specific tags (--exclude-notes-with-tags, --include-notes-with-tags)
  • Exclude or include the tags themself (--exclude-tags, --include-tags)

To check the exact command, visit the help page (--help).

docs.title: Limitations
docs.text: Filtering by notebooks is not possible, because nesting can yield some ambiguities.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# show the help
jimmy-cli-linux --help

# import specific nots only
jimmy-cli-linux obsidian_vault/ --format obsidian --include-notes "Sample note" "Second sample note"

# exclude notes with a tag
jimmy-cli-linux obsidian_vault/ --format obsidian --exclude-notes-with-tags "ignore this tag"

# don't import any tags
jimmy-cli-linux obsidian_vault/ --format obsidian --exclude-tags "*"

docs.title: Show the Note Tree
docs.text: The note tree in the intermediate format can be shown by --print-tree.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
1. Simply show the imported note tree:
$ jimmy-cli-linux .cache/arbitrary_folder/arbitrary_folder/ --print-tree
[07/28/24 16:50:51] INFO     Importing notes from ".cache/arbitrary_folder/arbitrary_folder"
                    INFO     Start parsing
[07/28/24 16:50:52] INFO     Finished parsing: 3 notebooks, 7 notes, 3 resources, 1 note links
Note Tree
└── 📘 2024-07-28 16:50:51 - Import from Joplin Custom Importer
    └── 📘 arbitrary_folder
        ├── 📖 sample
        │   ├── 🎴 test.png
        │   └── 🔗 link to second_sample_file
        ├── 📖 plaintext
        ├── 📖 test
        ├── 📖 Big-Fish
        └── 📘 nested_arbitrary_folder
            ├── 📖 LibreOffice Writer
            │   └── 🎴 1000000100000B5F00000449B7E060775BACC2F0.png
            ├── 📖 asciidoc
            │   └── 🎴 test\_image
            └── 📖 second_sample_file
                    INFO     Start filtering
                    INFO     Finished filtering: 3 notebooks, 7 notes, 3 resources, 1 note links
                    INFO     Start import to Joplin
                    INFO     Imported notes to Joplin successfully. Please verify that everything was imported.

Notebooks  100%|████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████| 3/3 [00:00<00:00]
Notes      100%|████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████| 7/7 [00:00<00:00]
Resources  100%|████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████| 3/3 [00:00<00:00]
Note Links 100%|████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████| 1/1 [00:00<00:00]
  1. Verify that the tags are really excluded:
$ jimmy-cli-linux .cache/obsidian/obsidian_vault --format obsidian --exclude-tags "*" --print-tree
[07/28/24 16:45:21] INFO     Importing notes from ".cache/obsidian/obsidian_vault"
                    INFO     Start parsing
                    INFO     Finished parsing: 2 notebooks, 2 notes, 3 resources, 4 tags, 4 note links
Note Tree
└── 📘 2024-07-28 16:45:21 - Import from obsidian
    ├── 📖 Sample note
    │   ├── 🎴 markdown image
    │   ├── 🔖 inline-tag-obsidian
    │   ├── 🔖 frontmatter-tag-obsidian
    │   ├── 🔖 another-tag-obsidian
    │   ├── 🔗 alias
    │   └── 🔗 internal link markdown syntax
    └── 📘 folder
        └── 📖 Second sample note
            ├── 🎴 test.png
            ├── 🎴 plaintext.txt
            ├── 🔖 inline-tag-obsidian
            ├── 🔗 Sample note
            └── 🔗 Sample note alias
                    INFO     Start filtering
                    INFO     Finished filtering: 2 notebooks, 2 notes, 3 resources, 4 note links
Note Tree Filtered
└── 📘 2024-07-28 16:45:21 - Import from obsidian
    ├── 📖 Sample note
    │   ├── 🎴 markdown image
    │   ├── 🔗 alias
    │   └── 🔗 internal link markdown syntax
    └── 📘 folder
        └── 📖 Second sample note
            ├── 🎴 test.png
            ├── 🎴 plaintext.txt
            ├── 🔗 Sample note
            └── 🔗 Sample note alias
                    INFO     Start import to Joplin
[07/28/24 16:45:22] INFO     Imported notes to Joplin successfully. Please verify that everything was imported.

Notebooks  100%|████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████| 2/2 [00:00<00:00]
Notes      100%|████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████| 2/2 [00:00<00:00]
Resources  100%|████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████| 3/3 [00:00<00:00]

docs.title: Contact
docs.text:
• General discussion at the Joplin forum
  • Report a bug or request a new feature at github

docs.title: How to add a new format?
docs.text:
Here is a good example commit.

The brief workflow is:

  1. Discuss the format at the forum or github
  2. Implement at src/
  3. Provide an example file and add a test
  4. Lint with lint.sh
  5. Document at docs/

docs.title: Donations
docs.text: I'm happy about a star on the github repository. If you really want to donate, please donate to the Joplin core team. They do great work.

docs.title: Why Joplin's data API is used?
docs.text:
• Plain markdown: Tags aren't supported.
  • JEX: Requires to work with some internals that I would rather not touch.
  • Joplin's data API: Straight forward to use and supports all needed functions.

docs.title: Why enlighten and not tqdm for progress bars?
docs.text: enlighten did integrate easier with pythons logging.

docs.title: Bear
docs.text: This page describes how to import notes from Bear to Joplin.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extension: .bear2bk

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Export as described in https://bear.app/faq/backup-restore/
  2. Install jimmy
  3. Import to Joplin. Example: jimmy-cli-linux backup.bear2bk --format bear

docs.title: Cacher
docs.text: This page describes how to import notes from Cacher to Joplin.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extension: .json

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Export as described in https://www.cacher.io/docs/guides/snippets/exporting-snippets#how-to-export-1
  2. Install jimmy
  3. Import to Joplin. Example: jimmy-cli-linux cacher-export-202406182304.json --format cacher

docs.title: Import Structure
docs.text:
• Cacher labels are converted to Joplin tags.
  • Cacher snippets are converted to Joplin notebooks.
  • Cacher files are converted to Joplin notes.

docs.title: Known Limitations
docs.text:
• Only markdown files are converted for now.
  • Files attached to snippets are not exported.

docs.title: CherryTree
docs.text: This page describes how to import notes from CherryTree to Joplin.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extension: .ctd

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Export as described in https://www.giuspen.com/cherrytreemanual/#_exporting
    1. Choose "Export" -> "Export to CherryTreeDocument" -> "All the Tree" -> "XML, Not Protected (.cdt)"
  2. Install jimmy
  3. Import to Joplin. Example: jimmy-cli-linux cherry.ctd --format cherrytree

docs.title: Import Structure
docs.text:
• CherryTree nodes are converted to Joplin notes.
  • If a CherryTree node contains sub nodes, a Joplin Notebook is created as container. It has the same name as the corresponding note.

docs.title: Known Limitations
docs.text:
• Cherrytree checked (☑) and crossed checkboxes (☒) are converted to markdown checked checkboxes ([x]).
  • Cherrytree latex is converted to a markdown code block.
  • Some cherrytree elements are not inserted at their original location, but at the end of the note. This is a limitation of cherrytree's export.
  • Cherrytree anchors are not converted.

docs.title: Clipto
docs.text: This page describes how to import notes from Clipto to Joplin.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extension: .json

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Export your notes from the mobile app [^export]
  2. Install jimmy
  3. Import to Joplin. Example: jimmy-cli-linux clipto_backup_240401_105154.json --format clipto

[^export]: Export works with the mobile app only. See https://github.com/clipto-pro/Desktop/issues/21#issuecomment-537401330.

docs.title: Day One
docs.text: This page describes how to import notes from Day One to Joplin.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extension: .zip

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Export as described in https://dayoneapp.com/guides/tips-and-tutorials/exporting-entries/
    1. Choose "Day One JSON (.zip)"
  2. Install jimmy
  3. Import to Joplin. Example: jimmy-cli-linux Export-Tagebuch.zip --format day_one

docs.title: Import Structure
docs.text:
• Each day is converted to a notebook.
  • Entries are converted to notes and grouped into the corresponding notebook of that day.
  • Referenced Audios, PDF, photos and videos are imported as resources.

docs.title: Known Limitations
docs.text:
• Unreferenced photos are not imported.
  • Photos that are references by multiple notes are only imported once (i. e. in one note). This seems to be a bug in the Day One export.

docs.title: Default Import
docs.text: This page describes how to import notes from single files or folders (recursively) to Joplin.

docs.title: Supported Formats
docs.text:
The default import covers the following formats:

  • Every format that is supported by pandoc. Some formats may need some tweaking, though. A few examples:
    • CSV
    • DocBook
    • docx
    • EPUB
    • HTML
    • Jupyter notebook
    • ODT
    • OPML
    • MediaWiki
    • reStructuredText
    • RTF
    • txt2tags
  • asciidoc (requires asciidoctor installed and in path)
  • Fountain (has a built-in Joplin plugin that needs to be activated in the settings)
  • Markdown (Commonmark)
  • txt

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Install jimmy
  2. Import to Joplin. Examples:
# import a single file supported by pandoc
jimmy-cli-linux libre_office_document.odt

# import multiple files
jimmy-cli-linux plaintext.txt markdown.md

# import all files in a folder recursively
jimmy-cli-linux path/to/folder

docs.title: Import Structure
docs.text:
• Folders are converted to Joplin notebooks
  • Files are converted to Joplin notes

docs.title: Dynalist
docs.text: This page describes how to import notes from Dynalist to Joplin.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extension: .zip

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Export as described in https://help.dynalist.io/article/79-back-up-your-data
  2. Install jimmy
  3. Import to Joplin. Example: jimmy-cli-linux dynalist-backup-2024-04-12.zip --format dynalist

docs.title: Facebook
docs.text: This page describes how to import posts and messages from Facebook to Joplin.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extension: .zip

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Export as described at this help page
    1. Choose JSON
    2. The export may take some time. For a 450 MB file it took two days.
  2. Install jimmy
  3. Import to Joplin. Example: jimmy-cli-linux facebook-xyz-07.07.2024-m9lv24pS.zip --format facebook

docs.title: Import Structure
docs.text:
• Posts and messages are stored in separate notebooks.
  • Each post is converted to a separate note, starting with the creation date (YYYY-MM-DD).
  • Each conversation is a note. Messages are concatenated inside the note and separated by the day. Conversations may be splitted to prevent too big notes.
  • Referenced resources (audio, gif, photos, videos and other files) are converted.

docs.title: Known Limitations
docs.text:
The import was tested with many messages (450 MB, >50000 messages) and only a few posts (~20 posts). The import may be not robust enough yet.

  • Shared posts are not imported.
  • Profile images, stories and shorts are not imported.
  • Group chats are not imported, since Facebook creates a new file everytime a person joins or leaves. It's not possible to merge them by ID.

docs.title: FuseBase
docs.text: This page describes how to import notes from FuseBase (formerly Nimbus Note) to Joplin.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extension: Folder with .zip files

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Export as described in https://nimbusweb.me/guides/settings/how-to-export-notes-to-html-or-pdf/
  2. Install jimmy
  3. Import to Joplin. Example: jimmy-cli-linux nimbus_note/Example/ --format nimbus_note

docs.title: Google Keep
docs.text: This page describes how to import notes from Google Keep to Joplin.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extensions: .zip, .tgz

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Export via Takeout as described in https://www.howtogeek.com/694042/how-to-export-your-google-keep-notes-and-attachments/
  2. Install jimmy
  3. Import to Joplin. Examples:
    1. jimmy-cli-linux takeout-20240401T160516Z-001.zip --format google_keep
    2. jimmy-cli-linux takeout-20240401T160556Z-001.tgz --format google_keep

docs.title: Joplin
docs.text: This page describes how to import notes in Joplin's JEX format to Joplin.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extension: .jex

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Export as described in https://joplinapp.org/help/apps/import_export/#exporting
  2. Install jimmy
  3. Import to Joplin. Example: jimmy-cli-linux 29_04_2024.jex --format joplin

docs.title: jrnl
docs.text: This page describes how to import notes from jrnl to Joplin.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extension: .json

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Export as described in https://jrnl.sh/en/stable/formats/#exporting-with-file
    1. Export as json to preserve metadata.
    2. Example: jrnl --format json --file myjournal.json
  2. Install jimmy
  3. Import to Joplin. Example: jimmy-cli-linux myjournal.json --format jrnl

docs.title: Import Structure
docs.text: Each journal entry is converted to a separate note in a flat notebook.

docs.title: Notion
docs.text: This page describes how to import notes from Notion to Joplin.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extension: .zip

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Export as described in https://www.notion.so/de-de/help/export-your-content
    1. Choose "Markdown and CSV" and uncheck "Create folder for sub-pages" when exporting.
  2. Install jimmy
  3. Import to Joplin. Example: jimmy-cli-linux 72a2f31c-3a46-4b44-826d-ae046e693551_Export-d609fb9f-43a4-475d-ba88-1db3e9e6bcd2.zip --format notion

docs.title: Obsidian
docs.text: This page describes how to import notes from Obsidian to Joplin.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extension: Folder with .md files

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Install jimmy
  2. Import to Joplin. Example: jimmy-cli-linux obsidian_vault/ --format obsidian

docs.title: Simplenote
docs.text: This page describes how to import notes from Simplenote to Joplin.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extension: .zip

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Export as described in https://simplenote.com/help/#export
  2. Install jimmy
  3. Import to Joplin. Example: jimmy-cli-linux notes.zip --format simplenote

docs.title: Standard Notes
docs.text: This page describes how to import notes from Standard Notes to Joplin.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extension: .zip

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Export as described in https://standardnotes.com/help/14/how-do-i-create-and-import-backups-of-my-standard-notes-data
  2. Install jimmy
  3. Import to Joplin. Example: jimmy-cli-linux "Standard Notes Backup - Sun Apr 28 2024 12_56_55 GMT+0200.zip" --format standard_notes

docs.title: Known Limitations
docs.text: Note links, attachments and folders are not implemented yet, since they require a subscription.

docs.title: Synology Note Station
docs.text: This page describes how to import notes from Synology Note Station to Joplin.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extension: .nsx

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Export as described in https://kb.synology.com/en-global/DSM/help/NoteStation/note_station_managing_notes?version=7#t7
  2. Install jimmy
  3. Import to Joplin. Example: jimmy-cli-linux notestation-test-books.nsx --format synology_note_station

docs.title: Known Limitations
docs.text:
If you have any of the following issuea and can provide an example file in .nsx format, please open an issue or send me an email.

  • Some tables aren't converted. See here.
  • Some resources aren't converted. See here.
  • Encrypted notes aren't converted. This would require to implement the decryption algorithm, which is not documented.

docs.title: Textbundle
docs.text: This page describes how to import notes from Textbundle to Joplin.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extensions: .textbundle, .textpack

docs.title: Apps that support Textbundle export
docs.text:
Selection from the textbundle website:

  • Bear
  • Craft
  • Ulysses
  • Wordpress
  • Zettlr

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Export from any of the mentioned apps
  2. Install jimmy
  3. Import to Joplin. Examples:
    1. jimmy-cli-linux "Textbundle Example v1.textbundle/" --format textbundle
    2. jimmy-cli-linux "example.textpack" --format textbundle

docs.title: Tiddlywiki
docs.text: This page describes how to import notes from TiddlyWiki to Joplin.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extension: .json

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Export as described in https://tiddlywiki.com/static/How%2520to%2520export%2520tiddlers.html
    1. Choose json export
  2. Install jimmy
  3. Import to Joplin. Example: jimmy-cli-linux tiddlers.json --format tiddlywiki

docs.title: Known Limitations
docs.text: Note content is imported in TiddlyWiki's WikiText format and not converted to markdown.

docs.title: Todo.txt
docs.text: This page describes how to import notes from Todo.txt to Joplin.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extension: .txt

docs.title: Apps that support Todo.txt
docs.text:
• The main todo.txt app
  • Other projects

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Export from any of the mentioned apps
  2. Install jimmy
  3. Import to Joplin. Example: jimmy-cli-linux examples_from_readme.txt --format todo_txt

docs.title: Todoist
docs.text: This page describes how to import notes from Todoist to Joplin.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extension: .csv

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Export as described in https://todoist.com/de/help/articles/introduction-to-backups-ywaJeQbN
    1. Uncheck "Use relative data" when exporting.
  2. Install jimmy
  3. Import to Joplin. Example: jimmy-cli-linux Privates.csv --format todoist

docs.title: Known Limitations
docs.text:
• Finished todo's are not exported at all.
  • Subtasks are converted to regular notes. I. e. they lose their indentation.
  • Markdown is not rendered in note titles.

docs.title: Tomboy-ng
docs.text: This page describes how to import notes from Tomboy-ng to Joplin.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extension: Folder with .note files

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Export as described in https://todoist.com/de/help/articles/introduction-to-backups-ywaJeQbN
    1. Uncheck "Use relative data" when exporting.
  2. Install jimmy
  3. Import to Joplin. Examples:
    1. jimmy-cli-linux ~/.local/share/tomboy-ng/ --format tomboy_ng
    2. jimmy-cli-linux tomboy-ng/5E74990A-E93E-4A11-AEA2-0814A37FEE1A.note --format tomboy_ng

docs.title: Known Limitations
docs.text:
• Note links are not exported.
  • Multiple formats are not converted properly.

docs.title: Toodledo
docs.text: This page describes how to import notes from Toodledo to Joplin.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extension: .csv

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Export as described in https://www.toodledo.com/tools/import_export.php
    1. Uncheck "Use relative data" when exporting.
  2. Install jimmy
  3. Import to Joplin. Examples:
    1. jimmy-cli-linux toodledo_completed_240427.csv --format toodledo
    2. jimmy-cli-linux toodledo_current_240427.csv --format toodledo
    3. jimmy-cli-linux toodledo_notebook_240427.csv --format toodledo

docs.title: Known Limitations
docs.text: subtasks and files are not implemented, since they require a subscription.

docs.title: xit
docs.text: This page describes how to import notes from [x]it! to Joplin.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extension: .xit

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Install jimmy
  2. Import to Joplin. Example: jimmy-cli-linux example.xit --format xit

docs.title: Import Structure
docs.text:
• xit groups are converted to Joplin notebooks
  • xit items are converted to Joplin todos
  • Priority, tags and due dates are converted to metadata and removed from the todo title

docs.title: Zoho Notebook
docs.text: This page describes how to import notes from Zoho Notebook to Joplin.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extension: .zip

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Export as described in https://help.zoho.com/portal/en/kb/notebook/import-and-export/articles/export-all-your-notecards-from-notebook
    1. Export as HTML
  2. Install jimmy
  3. Import to Joplin. Example: jimmy-cli-linux Notebook_14Apr2024_1300_html.zip --format zoho_notebook

docs.title: Known Limitations
docs.text: • Checklists are converted to plain lists. This might change with a newer pandoc version.
